question:
Will Lucky Day (Chuck Tingle) win Readers’ Favorite Horror in the 2025 Goodreads Choice Awards?

Description: The Goodreads Choice Awards are presented annually by Goodreads.com. For the 2025 Goodreads Choice Awards, the winners will be announced on December 4, 2025.

This market will resolve according to the listed book that wins the Readers' Favorite Horror award in the 2025 Goodreads Choice Awards.

If, for any reason, no winner is declared by December 31, 2025, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

In the case of a tie for the winner, this market will resolve in favor of the listed book that comes first in alphabetical order.

The resolution source will be the Goodreads website (https://www.goodreads.com/choiceawards/best-books-2025); however, other credible reporting may be used.

evidences.title: Voting has Begun for the 2025 Goodreads Choice Awards
evidences.content: View All posts by Community ### Share on: The opening round of the 2025 Goodreads Choice Awards has started, and it continues until November 23rd. Following that is the final round of voting, which will have only a shortlist of the most popular books from round one. That opens November 25th and runs until November 30th. The winners are announced December 4th. There are books in 15 categories to vote for: Fiction, Historical Fiction, Mystery & Thriller, Romance, Romantasy, Fantasy, Science Fiction, Horror, Debut Novel, Audiobook, Young Adult Fantasy & Sci-Fi, Young Adult Fiction, Nonfiction, Memoir, and History & Biography. evidences.title: Review: Lucky Day – The Speculative Shelf
evidences.content: # The Speculative Shelf ## Speculative fiction book reviews # Review: Lucky Day Title: Lucky Day Author: Chuck Tingle Pages: 240 Release: August 12, 2025 Mr. Tingle, I was not familiar with your game. What a wild ride this was. The blend of absurdism and horror is beautifully done. Tingle’s twisted imagination shines brightest in the breathless sequences of gore and surreal terror. There’s a scene in a wave pool so gruesome it will make me think twice before dipping my toes into the deep end ever again. Some of the broader, more otherworldly strokes – e.g., cosmic centipedes and literal voids in spacetime – kept me from fully embracing the premise. But the slick pacing ensures that no beat overstays its welcome, propelling the story forward with relentless energy. [...] Count me as a Tingle fan from here on out. This was a bloody good time. ★★★½ My thanks to the publisher and NetGalley for an advanced reader copy in exchange for an honest review. ### Share this: # Post navigation ### Leave a comment Cancel reply Δ Book Review 📖: Book Review 📓: Book Review 📙: It’s been 10 years to the day since I published my first review on The Speculative Shelf. 275 reviews later, much has chan...

evidences.title: All the New Horror Books Arriving in November 2025 - Reactor
evidences.content: Alone in a frontier town in the nineteenth-century Pacific Northwest, Gentle Montgomery is grieving his best friend. Liam was an alchemist, killed when he tried to capture a creature that shouldn’t exist: a giant salamander that drives men mad. When Gentle’s nephew, Kitt, arrives at his doorstep, the two set out together to track the monster down so they can use its blood in an alchemical formula that will bring Liam back to life. It’s a hard and haunted journey. The salamander produces surreal nightmares and waking dreams of a blighted, burning future. And Gentle and Kitt soon find themselves pursued by a bloodthirsty hunter, a sadistic judge, and a doomsday cult, all of whom have their own plans for the river monster. Armed with nothing but Liam’s alchemical notebooks, they must not [...] ### November 11 Bones of Our Stars, Blood of Our World— Cullen Bunn (Gallery Books) The bodies are stacking up on Wilson Island. The town’s sheriff has his suspicions but no genuine evidence for an arrest, even as the murders continue and appear increasingly ritualistic in nature. And when an arrest is finally made, all hell breaks loose—literally—as a terrifying horror rises to envelop the t...

evidences.title: Instagram
evidences.content: • Follow goodreads's profile picture goodreads Let's go behind the scenes of the 17th annual Goodreads Choice Awards with the Goodreads Editorial Team! ✨👋 Through lots of hard work, tireless analysis of readers' most popular books of 2025, and a trusty supply of snacks, it all comes together: 300 incredible nominees, 15 categories, and 2 rounds of voting – all chosen by YOU, the readers. The nominees are in, the categories are set – now it's time for YOU to vote for readers' favorite books of the year!⁠ ⁠ Vote on Goodreads.com, in the app, or click the link in bio!⁠ ⁠ 📖 Opening Round (Nov 11-23)⁠ 🗳️ Final Round (Nov 25-30)⁠ 🏆 Winners Announced! (Dec 4)⁠ ⁠ #GoodreadsChoice #GoodreadsChoiceAwards allbooktitles's profile picture allbooktitles ❤️ Reply

evidences.title: My 2025 Goodreads Choice Awards Predictions - YouTube
evidences.content: horror. It definitely has elements of both. It has horror in the name, so it's hard to know with that one, but I'm going to go ahead and maybe knock it out. It's generally kind of lower in the number of ratings and reviews that it has even over the span averaging out for days since publication. So, we'll say that one won't make it. I also don't know if this has any real credence to anything, but it's first shelved as horror on Goodreads. I feel like these probably just go in the order of number of times it's been shelved as the genre. I don't really know, but that makes it feel like maybe it will sit there more than it would in the mystery thriller. So, we're going to knock that one out. There's a couple shorter things like Death Row by Freda McFaten. Even though it's by Freda McFaten, [...] 🎥 RELATED VIDEOS All my GCA videos: TIME STAMPS 0:00 Intro 0:50 My stats 1:23 My methodology 3:04 My observations over the years 5:26 Spreadsheet overview 7:20 My top 20 prediction 20:58 My top 10 prediction 22:59 My winner prediction 24:53 My actual favorite mystery thriller books of 2025 25:35 What's coming next? #goodreads #booktube #books -- I love reading, connecting with other readers,...

evidences.title: Lucky Day | Washington Independent Review of Books
evidences.content: Lucky Day is, fundamentally, a rollickingly fun horror novel that is somehow jovial in its exploration of eldritch terrors and rips in the space-time continuum. It makes statistics fun. Its A+ cast of characters, including casino head Denver (a smooth-talking, no-bullshit woman cosplaying as a wealthy cowboy for no apparent reason), engaging Las Vegas locale, and commitment to the sci-fi bit make it a remarkable read. [...] At the center of the story is Tingle’s leading lady, Vera, a former statistics professor and survivor of the “Low-Probability Event” (LPE), a catastrophic nightmare that killed nearly 8 million people worldwide and resulted in the death of Vera’s mother and the dissolution of her engagement to her fiancée, Annie. The day of the disaster, fish rained from the sky, chimpanzees ran free in the streets of Chicago, and people spontaneously combusted in their living rooms. [...] Washington Independent Review of Books Logo # Lucky Day Statistics and climate catastrophe somehow add up to fun in this queer sci-fi/horror romp. Lucky Day Every now and then, a book feels like it was written with me in mind, improbably straddling my niche interests and life experience to...

evidences.title: Readers' Favorite Horror 2025 — Goodreads Choice Awards
evidences.content: The 2025 Goodreads Choice Awards have two rounds of voting open to registered Goodreads members. Winners are scheduled to be announced on December 4, 2025. The page lists 2025 rules and eligibility and includes books in the Readers' Favorite Horror category, such as Witchcraft for Wayward Girls by Grady Hendrix and Never Flinch by Stephen King.

evidences.title: Readers' Favorite Books 2025 — Goodreads Choice Awards
evidences.content: The Goodreads Choice Awards 2025 page shows the voting schedule and rules for the 2025 awards. It states that winners are scheduled to be announced on December 4, 2025, and includes links to previous years' awards and the 2025 rules and eligibility.

evidences.title: 2025 Rules & Eligibility - Goodreads
evidences.content: Books published in the United States in English, including translations and significant rereleases, between November 13, 2024, and November 11, 2025, are eligible for the 2025 Goodreads Choice Awards. Books published between November 12, 2025, and November 10, 2026, will be eligible for the 2026 awards. Goodreads says it uses site statistics to nominate 20 books in each category.

evidences.title: 2025 Rules & Eligibility - Goodreads
evidences.content: Goodreads states that the Choice Awards use two rounds of voting for registered members. Opening-round official nominees must have an average rating of 3.50 or higher at launch. A book may be nominated in no more than one genre category, and may also be nominated in Debut Novel and/or Audiobook.
